data.narrative:
Hermeus is a Series C supersonic and hypersonic technology company founded by AJ Piplica, Skyler Shuford, Glenn Case, and Mike Smayda. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $1.0B on $500M total capital raised. The broader comp universe for the sector is limited: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one identified private peer (Boom Supersonic) provide reference points, though the narrow peer set constrains conventional multiples-based approaches.

We applied stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers to support a reliable peer-median valuation. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B Series C post-money, yielding an implied valuation of $1.7B. This multiplier reflects typical milestone advancement and capital deployment between Series C and later institutional funding rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, with $1.7B as the central estimate. The range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression anchor, though the single-method foundation introduces material uncertainty. Key risks include the early-stage nature of commercial hypersonic development, limited comparable transaction data in the sector, and execution risk on development timelines. Investors should treat this range as provisional pending expansion of market comparables or emergence of clearer revenue/milestone data.
